name: Lentil Loaf
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons tomato paste or 3 cups tomato sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the rinsed lentils and bay leaf in a pot with 6 cups of water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer until the lentils are soft and the water has been absorbed, about 45 minutes.
2. In a medium saucepan, combine the bulgur and 2 cups of water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer until tender, about 15 minutes.
3. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
4. Transfer the cooked lentils to a large mixing bowl. Add the cooked bulgur and remaining ingredients except for the tomato paste or sauce. Mix well with your hands until thoroughly combined.
5. Pat the mixture into a 9-inch loaf pan. Bake for 40 minutes until firm but not dry.
6. During the last few minutes of baking, brush the top with tomato paste or sauce.
7. Let cool in the pan for 15 minutes before cutting into slices and serving warm.
This dish pairs well with mashed potatoes and your favorite vegetables. The leftovers taste great in a pita pocket, and if there are any mashed potatoes left over, you can stuff the pocket with both. Enjoy!
